title: Sanji Leaves the Baratie Behind
scene: On pages 9–12, the Baratie conflict focuses on Luffy, Sanji, Don Krieg, Gin, Zeff. After the fight, the Baratie shifts from survival to Sanji’s unresolved bond with Zeff and the cooks.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Ah that's right! I'm finally done with being an errand boy as of today!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: 'Cause I made that promise with the old man!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Right. Cong- rats.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: So then, you wanna...

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I ain't gonna be a pirate.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'm gonna stay here and be a cook.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Until the day that damn geezer acknowledges my cooking...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Fine, I'll give up then.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: But your hand hasn't given up!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: After something like that happening,

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I can't afford to leave even more.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The cooks here are completely independently.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: But I do want to go out to grand line one of these days.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Then let's go right now!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Not just yet though.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Hey...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Do you know about all blue?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: No.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: What? You've never heard of the ocean of miracles? Well you see, in all blue...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: talking with such a stupidly wide grin on your face...

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: Time for some grub, you bastards!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Baratie 2" floor, employee dining hall

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Hey, who's the one in charge of cooking today?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: That'd be me!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: And,

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Me!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Oh, the hellish duo? Guess it's nothing to look forward to then.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Just shut your traps and eat, you boiled-brain idiots!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Hm? Hey... where's our seats?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: Where's the food?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: The first time I was in the

summary: This part keeps the Baratie battle centered on willpower, debt, hunger, and pride. The dialogue shows who still stands firm after Krieg’s violence and who is ready to break away from the restaurant’s old life.
